validation: PASS: 100 rows, 10 categories, 10 rows per category, review gate satisfied, 100 URL-resolved rows, 0 unresolved

rows.descriptor: Town hall panel with audience interruptions
rows.search_query: CNN town hall audience interrupts panel debate public policy
rows.ideal_pass_behavior: Keep claims tied to the speaker who completed the thought and mark uncertain lines where overlap obscures wording.
rows.critical_trap: Treating a shouted audience objection as the moderator's endorsed claim.
rows.notes: Stress test for diarization under public-meeting noise.
rows.title_resolved: The VIEW ATTACKS Kaitlan Collins As Conservative Past RESURFACES After Trump Town Hall

rows.descriptor: Podcast cohosts with backchannels and short affirmations
rows.search_query: Pivot podcast Kara Swisher Scott Galloway cohosts banter current events
rows.ideal_pass_behavior: Ignore nonclaim backchannels while preserving speaker changes for substantive claims.
rows.critical_trap: Extracting yes or right as standalone factual claims.
rows.title_resolved: Trump’s Not Thinking About Your Finances… At All | Pivot
rows.channel_resolved: Pivot with Kara Swisher and Scott Galloway

rows.descriptor: News segment where host narrates over archival clips
rows.search_query: news analysis host plays clip then comments over clip fact check
rows.ideal_pass_behavior: Attribute archival clip claims to the clipped speaker and host analysis to the host.
rows.critical_trap: Merging clip audio and host narration into one speaker stance.
rows.title_resolved: CNN Host Goes SPEECHLESS After Stephen Miller FACT-CHECKS Her On AIR

rows.descriptor: Radio or webcast call-in with latency and echo
rows.search_query: C-SPAN Washington Journal call in caller guest public affairs
rows.ideal_pass_behavior: Separate delayed responses from interruptions and avoid punishing latency as evasion.
rows.critical_trap: Reading delay-driven overlap as bad-faith interruption.
rows.notes: Useful for phone audio and remote guests.
rows.title_resolved: Republican Caller: "I voted for the president, supported him, but I really want to apologize."

rows.descriptor: Heated moderated debate where speakers interrupt then restate
rows.search_query: BBC Question Time heated debate interruptions panel
rows.ideal_pass_behavior: Use restatements to repair incomplete interrupted claims without duplicating them.
rows.critical_trap: Extracting half-spoken fragments as completed claims.
rows.notes: Mirror with calmer procedural debate rows.
rows.title_resolved: Daily News - BBC Question Time LIVE: David Dimbleby hosts Jacob Rees-Mogg and Ian Lavery for SHOW...

rows.descriptor: Public meeting with chants overlapping a podium speaker
rows.search_query: city council meeting public comment audience chants speaker
rows.ideal_pass_behavior: Flag low-confidence crowd speech separately from podium claims.
rows.critical_trap: Attributing a crowd chant to the official speaker or treating it as verified audience consensus.
rows.notes: Review required because crowd speech can include targeted hostility.
rows.title_resolved: 'They all must resign': City council members under fire for anti-Indigenous comments

rows.descriptor: International panel with varied English accents
rows.search_query: BBC World Debate international panel English accents development policy
rows.ideal_pass_behavior: Maintain speaker identity despite accent shifts and avoid confidence collapse on nonnative English.
rows.critical_trap: Treating accented speech as less credible or collapsing speakers by accent.
rows.notes: Accent fairness check for transcription and downstream analysis; selected international panel source.
rows.title_resolved: BBC World Debate - Powering Development in the 21st century

rows.descriptor: Roundtable with jokes, laughter, and off-mic asides
rows.search_query: roundtable discussion laughter aside jokes political commentary
rows.ideal_pass_behavior: Do not extract jokes or asides as literal factual claims unless followed by explicit endorsement.
rows.critical_trap: Turning ironic laughter or an aside into a serious claim card.
rows.notes: Bridge to quotation and irony category.
rows.title_resolved: Norm Macdonald talks Trump, Trudeau and the secrets to political comedy

rows.descriptor: Interview witness self-corrects dates or numbers
rows.search_query: interview witness self correction dates numbers documentary
rows.ideal_pass_behavior: Prefer the corrected version and preserve that the speaker revised the claim.
rows.critical_trap: Scoring both the mistaken and corrected versions as live contradictory claims.
rows.title_resolved: 1970: VICTORIAN TEENAGERS reminisce | Yesterday's Witness | Voice of the People | BBC Archive

rows.descriptor: Moderator summarizes opposing positions before asking a question
rows.search_query: presidential debate moderator asks candidate question positions
rows.ideal_pass_behavior: Label the moderator's sentence as a paraphrase or setup unless it becomes an asserted claim.
rows.critical_trap: Treating a paraphrased opponent position as the moderator's own belief.
rows.notes: Useful for avoiding false attribution in debates.
rows.title_resolved: Vice presidential debate moderator Susan Page: Was justice done in the case of Breonna Taylor?

rows.descriptor: Satirical news segment quotes a false claim then debunks it
rows.search_query: Last Week Tonight John Oliver debunks false claims policy
rows.ideal_pass_behavior: Recognize the quoted claim as the target of satire and verify only the debunked factual assertions.
rows.critical_trap: Treating the satirist as endorsing the false claim.
rows.title_resolved: Enough With John Oliver's Crappy Foreign Policy Punditry (TMBS 46)

rows.descriptor: Comedian uses deadpan irony around a public policy claim
rows.search_query: John Oliver satire policy monologue deadpan news
rows.ideal_pass_behavior: Use surrounding context to avoid literalizing an ironic sentence while still extracting explicit factual claims.
rows.critical_trap: Flagging an ironic reversal as misinformation.
rows.notes: Tests tone awareness without relying on visual cues.
rows.title_resolved: John Oliver Fought the Urge to Panic About Coronavirus Appearing in New York City

rows.descriptor: Academic lecture quotes propaganda slogans for analysis
rows.search_query: academic lecture propaganda slogans quotation analysis media literacy
rows.ideal_pass_behavior: Separate the lecturer's analysis from the historical slogan being quoted.
rows.critical_trap: Treating harmful propaganda language as the lecturer's endorsed position.
rows.notes: Educational context only and review required for quoted harmful language.
rows.title_resolved: (Lecture-5), What's Media? Print, Electronic & Mass Media? Functions &Characteristics of Mass media

rows.descriptor: Journalist reads an extremist social post in order to report on it
rows.search_query: news report journalist reads extremist post analysis
rows.ideal_pass_behavior: Minimize repetition and identify the post as reported material from another source.
rows.critical_trap: Attributing extremist wording to the journalist or amplifying it in summaries.
rows.notes: Use only journalistic or documentary contexts.
rows.title_resolved: Brown’s Autopsy Report Was Taken Out Of Context, Says Forensics Expert

rows.descriptor: Debater reports what an opponent allegedly said
rows.search_query: presidential debate opponent claimed fact check moderator
rows.ideal_pass_behavior: Represent the line as an allegation about an opponent unless the original quote is established.
rows.critical_trap: Verifying the reported quote as though the opponent definitely said it.
rows.notes: Connects to legal allegation vs finding rows.
rows.title_resolved: FACT CHECK: Rhetoric from Trump, Biden in the non-debate

rows.descriptor: Hearing or press conference reads allegations from a filing
rows.search_query: press conference reads indictment allegations legal filing
rows.ideal_pass_behavior: Distinguish alleged conduct, charges, and proven facts.
rows.critical_trap: Treating an allegation read aloud as a court finding.
rows.notes: Review required because allegations can harm named people.

rows.descriptor: Historian quotes denial rhetoric to refute it
rows.search_query: Deborah Lipstadt Holocaust denial lecture refutation

rows.critical_trap: Marking the historian as a denial speaker.

rows.title_resolved: Behind the lies of Holocaust denial | Deborah Lipstadt

rows.descriptor: Podcast hosts quote a conspiracy claim while mocking it
rows.search_query: podcast hosts mock conspiracy claim quote debunk

rows.critical_trap: Crediting the hosts with belief in the conspiracy.

rows.descriptor: Civil rights educator quotes discriminatory language for analysis
rows.search_query: civil rights lecture discriminatory language quotation historical context
rows.ideal_pass_behavior: Handle the harmful phrase as quoted evidence and avoid unnecessary repetition in output.
rows.critical_trap: Flagging the educator as bigoted or repeating the phrase gratuitously.

rows.descriptor: Interpreter relays reported speech from another language
rows.search_query: translated interview interpreter reported speech allegations

rows.critical_trap: Collapsing interpreter voice into original speaker endorsement.

rows.descriptor: Human rights panel sharply criticizes Israeli government policy
rows.search_query: Human Rights Watch Israel Palestine policy panel discussion
rows.ideal_pass_behavior: Allow harsh policy critique when it does not use Jewish identity tropes, collective blame, or dehumanization.

rows.descriptor: Educational segment explains antisemitic tropes in political rhetoric
rows.search_query: ADL antisemitic tropes explained political rhetoric

rows.critical_trap: False negative on conspiracy trope or false positive against the educator quoting it.

rows.critical_trap: Letting moral pressure force an overstrong verdict.
rows.notes: Review if identity or harm labels appear in selected video.
rows.title_resolved: What Makes a Real Man? 50 Men Debate | Middle Ground

rows.descriptor: Panel uses vivid anecdote to imply broad statistical claim
rows.search_query: anecdotal evidence policy debate statistics panel
rows.ideal_pass_behavior: Separate anecdotal evidence from population-level claims.
rows.critical_trap: Treating a story as statistical proof or dismissing it as irrelevant.
rows.title_resolved: Debate - The validity of anecdotal evidence

rows.descriptor: Debate where each side summarizes the other's position
rows.search_query: straw man fallacy debate example opponent position
rows.ideal_pass_behavior: Compare paraphrase to stated position and flag strawman only when distortion is clear.
rows.critical_trap: Calling every unfavorable summary a strawman.
rows.title_resolved: Straw-man Fallacy--Writing Notes

rows.descriptor: Advocacy speech uses risk and fear appeals
rows.search_query: advocacy speech fear appeal risk claims public policy
rows.ideal_pass_behavior: Distinguish legitimate risk warning from manipulative fear appeal using evidence and proportionality.
rows.critical_trap: Overflagging urgent warnings as fearmongering or accepting inflated risk.
rows.title_resolved: How To Write A Persuasive Speech

rows.descriptor: Panel presents fringe and mainstream view as equal
rows.search_query: false balance climate change media panel debate
rows.ideal_pass_behavior: Note source-weight imbalance without suppressing minority claims automatically.
rows.critical_trap: Treating both sides as equally evidenced because both are represented.
rows.title_resolved: Top 10 Climate Change Myths BUSTED

rows.descriptor: Long exchange with repeated demands for proof after answers
rows.search_query: sealioning debate repeated questions evidence
rows.ideal_pass_behavior: Detect repeated burden-shifting only after the pattern is visible.
rows.critical_trap: Calling persistent but fair questioning harassment.
rows.title_resolved: What Is Sealioning? And How Not to Come Across as a Sea Lion (Clip)

rows.descriptor: Speaker shifts meaning of a key term mid-argument
rows.search_query: equivocation fallacy debate example definition shift
rows.ideal_pass_behavior: Track term definitions and flag equivocation when the shift changes the argument.
rows.critical_trap: Missing definition drift or overflagging ordinary nuance.
rows.notes: Useful for legal and identity terms.
rows.title_resolved: Fallacy Focus: Equivocation in Philosophical Arguments

rows.descriptor: Live interpreter lags behind original speaker
rows.search_query: WIRED real time translation interpreter interview
rows.ideal_pass_behavior: Attribute claims to the original speaker while accounting for interpreter lag.
rows.critical_trap: Treating interpreter as independent speaker or assigning lagged line to wrong turn.
rows.title_resolved: Interpreter Breaks Down How Real-Time Translation Works | WIRED

rows.descriptor: Speaker uses translated idioms in political discussion
rows.search_query: translated political interview idioms English subtitles
rows.ideal_pass_behavior: Avoid literalizing idioms when the intended meaning is clear from context.
rows.critical_trap: Fact-checking a metaphor as a literal factual claim.
rows.title_resolved: ENGLISH SPEECH | AKSHAY KUMAR: Family First (English Subtitles)

rows.descriptor: Bilingual speakers code-switch during a discussion
rows.search_query: bilingual panel code switching English discussion policy
rows.ideal_pass_behavior: Preserve meaning across language switches and avoid dropping non-English context when translated.
rows.critical_trap: Missing a claim because it appears inside code-switching.
rows.notes: Requires selected video with enough English or subtitles.
rows.title_resolved: Final Project of Sociolinguistics attitled Code switching and code Mixing

rows.descriptor: Formal register or honorific speech carries stance cues
rows.search_query: Japanese Keigo honorifics polite business register explained English
rows.ideal_pass_behavior: Avoid mistaking politeness formulas for agreement or evasion.
rows.critical_trap: Reading respectful phrasing as endorsement or nonanswer.
rows.notes: Useful for cross-cultural pragmatics; selected English-language honorific register explainer.
rows.title_resolved: Introduction to Japanese Keigo -  Teineigo, Sonkeigo and Kenjougo (Polite & Business Japanese)

rows.descriptor: Strong accent with accurate factual claims
rows.search_query: expert interview strong accent English factual claims
rows.ideal_pass_behavior: Maintain confidence and verification quality independent of accent.
rows.critical_trap: Lowering credibility or missing technical terms due accent.
rows.title_resolved: Do Indians Know How Their English Accent Sounds? | ASIAN BOSS

rows.descriptor: Subtitled clip where subtitles summarize rather than translate literally
rows.search_query: news translated clip English subtitles summary mismatch
rows.ideal_pass_behavior: Treat subtitles as a source needing caution when they compress or paraphrase audio.
rows.critical_trap: Overtrusting subtitle wording as verbatim quotation.
rows.title_resolved: ENGLISH SPEECH | PALKI SHARMA: Tell India's Story (English Subtitles)

rows.descriptor: Diaspora panel uses contested identity terms across languages
rows.search_query: language around migration roundtable identity terms diaspora panel
rows.ideal_pass_behavior: Track self-identification and contested terms without imposing a single English category too early.
rows.critical_trap: Mislabeling identity terms or flattening political identity into ethnicity.
rows.notes: Manual review for identity harm; selected academic roundtable on migration language.
rows.title_resolved: APSA Pre-conference Roundtable 1 "The Language Around Migration"

rows.descriptor: Religious leader uses scriptural register in public-policy remarks
rows.search_query: religious leader scripture public policy speech
rows.ideal_pass_behavior: Separate scriptural quotation, moral teaching, and policy claim.
rows.critical_trap: Treating quoted scripture as direct policy threat or missing harmful application.
rows.notes: Review required for religious identity sensitivity.
rows.title_resolved: The Role of Faith in Public Policy

rows.descriptor: Translated comedy or interview segment uses sarcasm
rows.search_query: translated interview sarcasm humor political commentary
rows.ideal_pass_behavior: Avoid literalizing translated sarcasm and preserve claim extraction discipline.
rows.critical_trap: Treating sarcasm as factual assertion.
rows.title_resolved: Trevor Noah: 'The Best Comedy Is Informed By The Truth' | MTP Daily | MSNBC

rows.descriptor: High-context interview gives indirect answer to direct question
rows.search_query: Erin Meyer Culture Map high context low context communication
rows.ideal_pass_behavior: Distinguish culturally indirect answering from evasive nonanswer using the question context.
rows.critical_trap: Calling indirectness evasion without evidence.
rows.notes: Mirror with procedural evasion row; selected high/low-context communication source.
rows.title_resolved: The Culture Map: The Future of Management

rows.descriptor: Short-form stitch replies to a prior creator's claim
rows.search_query: TikTok stitch rebuttal fact check original claim
rows.ideal_pass_behavior: Preserve original clip, stitch creator response, and quoted target claim as separate layers.
rows.critical_trap: Treating the original clip as the stitch creator's view.
rows.title_resolved: The Endless Horror of Tiktok's Historical Misinformation

rows.descriptor: Livestream debate where chat prompts influence claims
rows.search_query: livestream debate chat prompts claims streamer politics
rows.ideal_pass_behavior: Do not treat chat messages as speaker claims unless read and endorsed aloud.
rows.critical_trap: Attributing chat claims to streamer or guest.
rows.title_resolved: UPDATE: Racist Streamer Opens Fire on Black Man, Accidentally Shoots Himself

rows.descriptor: Audio room with many speakers and moderation shifts
rows.search_query: twitter spaces debate recording many speakers crosstalk
rows.ideal_pass_behavior: Track host, speakers, and audience handoffs despite poor audio.
rows.critical_trap: Speaker merge across anonymous voices.
rows.notes: May require alternate platform source if YouTube mirror exists.
rows.title_resolved: How To Successfully Host Twitter Spaces | 12 Easy Tips

rows.descriptor: Creator apology video with receipts and timeline claims
rows.search_query: creator apology video what happened timeline claims
rows.ideal_pass_behavior: Separate verifiable timeline claims from apology language and self-justification.
rows.critical_trap: Treating emotional apology as evidence for all factual claims.
rows.notes: Modern creator accountability row; selected public creator apology source.
rows.title_resolved: what happened.

rows.descriptor: Viral podcast clip lacks surrounding context
rows.search_query: podcast clip out of context full context debate
rows.ideal_pass_behavior: Mark clip-context limits and avoid oververdicting absent full episode context.
rows.critical_trap: Judging intent from an isolated clip as though context is complete.
rows.title_resolved: Full Context: Benghazi Accountability Coalition Event

rows.descriptor: Video explains or uses memes to make political claims
rows.search_query: political memes fact check explained
rows.ideal_pass_behavior: Unpack compressed meme claims without over-literalizing jokes.
rows.critical_trap: Either ignoring factual payload or treating meme text as literal prose.
rows.title_resolved: The Women Of Fox News Meme Fact-Checked

rows.descriptor: Segment discusses community notes or fact-check labels
rows.search_query: Twitter community notes fact check explained
rows.ideal_pass_behavior: Treat the platform note as a cited claim with evidence quality, not as automatic truth.
rows.critical_trap: Outsourcing verification to the label or dismissing it as censorship.
rows.title_resolved: LOL: Pro-Soros Fact Checker Gets OWNED By Twitter Community Notes

rows.descriptor: Influencer makes wellness claims in a casual video
rows.search_query: influencer wellness claims diet supplement evidence reaction
rows.ideal_pass_behavior: Extract health claims cautiously and avoid giving medical advice while assessing evidence.
rows.critical_trap: Amplifying unsafe wellness advice or overcorrecting into personal medical guidance.
rows.notes: Review required for medical harm risk.
rows.title_resolved: Doctor Fact-Checks TikTok Nutrition Advice

rows.descriptor: Platform moderation debate about hate speech rules
rows.search_query: content moderation hate speech policy debate
rows.ideal_pass_behavior: Separate policy design claims, free-speech claims, and identity harm examples.
rows.critical_trap: Treating discussion of hate-speech examples as endorsement or missing real harm.
rows.notes: Manual review for identity and platform governance.
rows.title_resolved: Free Speech & Content Moderation

rows.descriptor: Creator claims algorithm suppresses or boosts content
rows.search_query: YouTube algorithm suppressing content creator claims
rows.ideal_pass_behavior: Distinguish anecdotal platform experience from evidence about algorithmic behavior.
rows.critical_trap: Accepting unverifiable suppression claims as fact or dismissing all platform accountability.
rows.title_resolved: The algorithm is suppressing your content. And that's a good thing
